key_insight: Doeblin curves generalize Doeblin coefficients into a nonlinear, divergence-aware contraction profile that can certify information contraction even when the classic coefficient is zero.
engineering_relevance: This provides sharper guarantees for noisy optimization, robust computation, and differential privacy analysis in iterative algorithms and circuits.
method: Define a Doeblin curve as a nonlinear map over divergence and power levels for a Markov kernel.
Develop a variational characterization of Doeblin coefficients and derive upper/lower bounds for the curves.
Apply the framework to generalization, noisy circuit reliability, and privacy guarantees in broader settings.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical analysis and bound derivations, with applications demonstrating broader-domain guarantees rather than a new experimental dataset.
watch_item: The framework appears mathematically powerful but may be hard to compute or validate tightly for complex channels, especially when only coarse kernel information is available.
